title: How to become hvac
body:
# HOW TO BECOME AN HVAC TECHNICIAN IN ONTARIO?

![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/11062b_57eee0be03fd4f26b8819bc60ef5b3c9f000.jpg/v1/fill/w_1018,h_40,al_c,q_80,usm_0.33_1.00_0.00,enc_avif,quality_auto/11062b_57eee0be03fd4f26b8819bc60ef5b3c9f000.jpg)

HVAC technicians play a crucial role in maintaining indoor comfort and air quality by ensuring heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems are running efficiently. If you're looking to pursue a career in this essential trade in Ontario.

 

Here's a step-by-step guide on how to get started:

## 01.

#### Complete Your High School Diploma or Equivalent

 

The first step toward becoming an HVAC technician is obtaining your high school diploma or an equivalent qualification. A strong foundation in subjects like math, physics, and electronics will be helpful as you move forward in your training.

## 02.

#### Enroll in a Pre-Apprenticeship Training Program

 

Once you have your diploma, enrolling in a pre-apprenticeship program is a great next step. Look for reputable trade schools like QuickFast Service Academy, which offers hands-on training and theoretical knowledge to set you up for success in the HVAC field.

## 03.

#### In Ontario, HVAC technicians typically specialize in one of two areas:

​​

- Refrigeration and Air Conditioning Systems Mechanic

- Residential Air Conditioning Systems Mechanic

​

Your choice will guide your training, apprenticeship, and career path.

## 04.

#### Gain Paid Work Experience Through Apprenticeship

 

To gain practical, on-the-job experience, you'll need to work under the guidance of a licensed professional. Apprenticeships combine paid work with classroom instruction, allowing you to apply what you’ve learned in real-world settings.

## 05.

#### Become a Licensed HVAC Professional

 

After completing your apprenticeship, you’ll need to take the qualifying exam to become a licensed HVAC technician in Ontario. Depending on your career path, you may also choose to take additional training as a Gas Technician, which can further expand your skill set and marketability.

## 06.

#### Earn Your Red Seal Certification

 

To take your credentials even further, consider earning your Red Seal Certification. This certification is available for the Refrigeration and Air Conditioning Systems Mechanic (313A) trade and allows you to work anywhere across Canada as a fully qualified technician.

#### HVAC TECHNICIAN SALARY IN CANADA

The earning potential for HVAC technicians in Canada—especially in Ontario—can vary significantly based on your experience, specialization, and certifications. With proper training and licensing, many HVAC professionals can earn \$75,000 to \$90,000+ per year, with even higher salaries in management or specialized roles.

​

Here’s a breakdown of average annual salaries for common HVAC roles:

- HVAC Project Manager – \$90,000+\
  Oversees the planning, coordination, and execution of HVAC installations or service projects, often managing teams and budgets.

- HVAC Controls Technician – \$83,000+\
  Specializes in setting up and maintaining control systems for HVAC equipment, including automation and building management systems (BMS).

- HVAC Supervisor – \$82,000+\
  Leads teams of technicians, ensuring projects are completed on time and according to safety and quality standards.

- Refrigeration Systems Technician – \$75,000+\
  Focuses on installation and repair of commercial and industrial refrigeration systems, such as walk-in coolers, freezers, and chillers.

- HVAC Technician – \$75,000+\
  Handles general service, repair, and installation of heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems in residential, commercial, or industrial settings.

- HVAC Installer – \$68,000+\
  Specializes in installing HVAC units and ductwork, primarily in new construction or renovation projects.

​

 

​

​​​​​

#### Career Growth & Specialization Opportunities

The HVAC industry continues to grow with increasing demand for energy-efficient, smart, and environmentally friendly systems. As a certified technician, you’ll have the opportunity to:

​

- Move into project management or supervisory roles

- Specialize in refrigeration, energy efficiency, or automation

- Expand your skills with Gas Technician licensing or Red Seal certification

- Start your own HVAC business

​

With ongoing training and dedication, HVAC professionals can build long-term, high-paying careers across Canada.

![\_HVAC TECHNICIAN SALARY IN CANADA do me photos of men in blue collar canada.jpg](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/9fe3e0_10560f714c1145d3842de85064256375~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_717,h_1024,al_c,q_85,enc_avif,quality_auto/9fe3e0_10560f714c1145d3842de85064256375~mv2.jpg)

## Why Choose a Career as an HVAC Technician?

#### As many experienced tradespeople prepare to retire over the next five to ten years, the demand for skilled workers especially HVAC technicians is rapidly increasing. With the growing need for energy-efficient climate systems, the HVAC industry is full of opportunity.\
​\
This rising demand comes with competitive salaries, strong job security, and excellent opportunities for advancement. There has never been a better time to begin your career in HVAC.
